coredata.dc:title: A versatile assay for alkaline phosphatase detection based on thymine-HgII-thymine structure generation mediated by TdT
coredata.dc:creator: Wang, Yuanyuan
Yang, Li
Li, Nan
Sun, Chuankai
Xia, Yiping
Yuan, Libo
Lu, Jie
Liu, Fenyong
Xing, Xiwen
coredata.dc:description: Alkaline phosphatase (ALP) is a vital hydrolysis enzyme in phosphate metabolism, which catalyzes the hydrolysis of phosphate ester groups in proteins, nucleic acids, and other small molecules. Meanwhile, abnormal ALP expression is associated with occurrence and development of many diseases. Terminal deoxynucleotidyl transferase (TdT) is a widely used tool enzyme in many fields, which randomly adds deoxyribonucleoside triphosphates (dNTPs) at the 3′-OH termini of ssDNA in a template-free manner. In this work, we designed a versatile, convenient, label-free, and highly sensitive fluorescence enhancing assay for ALP activity detection based on the characteristics of ALP, TdT, and thymine-HgII-thymine (T-Hg2+-T) structure. In the presence of ALP, the 3′-phosphoryl end of the ssDNA-p was hydrolyzed to hydroxyl group, followed by addition of a poly-T tail on its 3′ terminal hydroxyl in the mixing solution containing both TdT and dTTPs. Then, the DNA with poly-T tail could interact with Hg2+ to form the stable T-Hg2+-T mediated metallo DNA duplex, which enhanced the fluorescence intensity of the SG. Under optimal conditions, the proposed system was employed for quantitatively monitoring ALP activity with a dynamic range of 0–2500 mU mL−1, and the actual detection limit could be down to 0.025 mU mL−1. And the determination of ALP activity in human serum samples and MCF-7 cells lysates exhibited a good sensing performance, demonstrating its potential applications in biochemical research and clinical diagnosis. Meanwhile, this system could also be applied to both TdT and Hg2+ detection.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Alkaline phosphatase
Terminal deoxynucleotidyl transferase
T-Hg2+-T structure
Fluorescence enhancement

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Selectivity and inhibition of the ALP detection assay
Assay of ALP activity in human serum and cell lysates samples
Principle and feasibility of the developed assay
Optimization of performance conditions for accurately ALP detection
Selectivity and inhibitor screening of ALP
ALP activity assay in biological fluid
Further application for TdT and Hg2+ detection

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: (a) Fluorescence emission spectra of DNA-P + Hg2+ (line 1), DNA-P + TdT + Hg2+ (line 2), DNA-P + ALP + Hg2+ (line 3), DNA-P + ALP + TdT (line 4), DNA-P + ALP + TdT + Hg2+ (line 5). All samples were stained by SG. (b) The AND logic scheme and truth table.

(a) Fluorescence emission spectra responses of the system toward different concentrations of ALP (From bottom to up, the signal were represent the sample with 0, 0.025, 0.05, 0.25, 0.5, 2.5, 5, 50, 250, 500, 2500 mU mL−1 ALP, respectively). (b) The relationship between the fluorescence enhancement and the ALP concentration from 0.025 to 2500 mU mL−1. The inset showed the responses of the system to ALP at low concentrations. The error bars represent standard deviation of three independently repetitive experiments.

(a) Selectivity of the assay for ALP detection. The concentration of ALP and other interference enzymes were 2.5 mU mL−1and 20 mU mL−1, respectively. (b) Fluorescence spectra toward 2.5 U mL−1 ALP pretreated with different final concentrations of Na3VO4 (0, 0.25, 2.5, 25, 40, 50, 75, 100, 125 μM). (c) Plot of the inhibition efficiency versus Na3VO4 concentration. The incubation time was 20 min. The error bars represent standard deviation of three repetitive experiments.

(a) The relationship between the fluorescence enhancement and different concentrations of cells lysates (2–40 μg mL−1). (b) Fluorescence intensity of ALP in MCF-7 (60 μg mL−1) with the presence or absence of Na3VO4 (100 μM). The error bars represent standard deviation of three repetitive experiments.

(a) The relationship between the fluorescence enhancement and the quantities of TdT from 0.01 to 10 U. The inset showed the responses of the assay to TdT at low quantities. (b) Selectivity of the assay for TdT detection. (c) The relationship between the fluorescence enhancement and the quantities of Hg2+ from 125 to 1250 nM. (d) Selectivity of the assay for Hg2+ detection. The error bars represent standard deviation of three repetitive experiments.
Schematic diagram of the versatile monitoring of ALP activity, TdT activity and Hg2+.

head.ce:title: A versatile assay for alkaline phosphatase detection based on thymine-HgII-thymine structure generation mediated by TdT

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Department of Biotechnology, College of Life Science and Technology, Jinan University Guangzhou, 510632, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Department of Biotechnology, College of Life Science and Technology, Jinan University Guangzhou

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: College of Life Science and Technology, Jinan University, Guangzhou 510632, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: College of Life Science and Technology, Jinan University

ce:footnote.ce:note-para: These authors contribute equally to this work.

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Alkaline phosphatase (ALP) is a vital hydrolysis enzyme in phosphate metabolism, which catalyzes the hydrolysis of phosphate ester groups in proteins, nucleic acids, and other small molecules. Meanwhile, abnormal ALP expression is associated with occurrence and development of many diseases. Terminal deoxynucleotidyl transferase (TdT) is a widely used tool enzyme in many fields, which randomly adds deoxyribonucleoside triphosphates (dNTPs) at the 3′-OH termini of ssDNA in a template-free manner. In this work, we designed a versatile, convenient, label-free, and highly sensitive fluorescence enhancing assay for ALP activity detection based on the characteristics of ALP, TdT, and thymine-HgII-thymine (T-Hg2+-T) structure. In the presence of ALP, the 3′-phosphoryl end of the ssDNA-p was hydrolyzed to hydroxyl group, followed by addition of a poly-T tail on its 3′ terminal hydroxyl in the mixing solution containing both TdT and dTTPs. Then, the DNA with poly-T tail could interact with Hg2+ to form the stable T-Hg2+-T mediated metallo DNA duplex, which enhanced the fluorescence intensity of the SG. Under optimal conditions, the proposed system was employed for quantitatively monitoring ALP activity with a dynamic range of 0–2500 mU mL−1, and the actual detection limit could be down to 0.025 mU mL−1. And the determination of ALP activity in human serum samples and MCF-7 cells lysates exhibited a good sensing performance, demonstrating its potential applications in biochemical research and clinical diagnosis. Meanwhile, this system could also be applied to both TdT and Hg2+ detection.

ce:section.ce:para: Alkaline phosphatase (ALP) is a hydrolase, which is responsible for the dephosphorylation process of nucleic acids, proteins, some other small molecules [1], and has been prevalently found in a variety of mammalian tissues, such as liver, bone, kidney, placenta, and intestine [2,3]. According to previous reports, the aberrant expression of ALP is related to many diseases, such as liver dysfunction [4], bone diseases [5], diabetes [6], and prostatic cancer [7], which make it a fine diagnostic biomarker. Moreover, it is also one of the most common markers in enzyme immunoassays, gene assays, histochemical staining, and related affinity sensing methods for monitoring proteins, nucleic acids, drugs, enzymes, and other analytes [8–12]. In environmental biology field, ALP is widely used as an index parameter of phosphate radical (PO4 3-) in phytoplankton phosphorus limitation. And ALP also have regulation ability to phosphorus concentration for seasonal micro-organism growth [13,14]. Therefore, a versatile, lable-free, convenient and sensitive method for ALP activity assay is of great importance for diagnostic, analytical applications and environmental biological analysis.

Hitherto, various methods have been applied to monitor ALP activity, including fluorescence [15–18], chromatographic analysis [19,20], colorimetry [21–24], surface-enhanced raman scattering [25,26], electrochemistry [27,28], and electrochemiluminescence [29,30]. Among them, fluorescence assays have several advantages, such as high sensitivity, selectivity, fast analysis, simple operation, being nonsample-destructive, and easy readout. Some ALP detection fluorescence probes, such as conjugated polyelectrolytes [31,32], small molecule organic probes [16,33], metal nanoclusters [34,35], nanosheets [36], and quantum dots (QDs) [37,38], are plagued with time consuming operation steps or involving toxic or expensive regents.

Terminal deoxynucleotidyl transferase (TdT), a template-free polymerase, could randomly add deoxyribonucleoside triphosphates (dNTPs) to 3′-termini of ssDNA. It is widely used into fast detection of target DNA, RNA, metal ions, DNA modifying enzymes, and apoptosis cells [39–47]. What's more, it is also essential for cancer-related pharmaceutical development and fundamental biochemical research. Mercury, another important component in our system, is a highly toxic and widely spread heavy metal pollutant, which can cause serious damage to human organs, such as the brain, kidneys, stomach, intestine, etc [48–51]. According to previous work, ssDNA with rich thymines can form stable DNA duplex with Hg2+ via thymine-HgII-thymine (T-Hg2+-T) structure [52], and this T-Hg2+-T structure has been employed to various detection methods, especially for Hg2+ detection [53]. Fortunately, SYBR Green I (SG), a DNA intercalating dye with low cost, has a significantly enhanced fluorescence in complex with T-Hg2+-T mediated metallo DNA duplex, but stains single-stranded DNA (ssDNA) with weak performance. Thus, it is widely used to develop label-free fluorescent sensors for biomolecular detection [54].

In this work, we developed a novel label-free fluorescence enhancing assay for ALP activity detection based on the unique T-Hg2+-T structure. The schematic illustration of the design route for the detection of ALP is presented in Scheme 1. Firstly, the 3′-phosphorylated ssDNA (ssDNA-p) served as a DNA probe, and 3′-phosphoryl end of ssDNA-p was dephosphorylated by ALP. Then, TdT catalyzed the sequential addition of deoxynucleotides (dTTPs) at 3′-OH end of ssDNA to extend the probe with a poly-T sequence. The poly-T tailed ssDNA probe could form stable DNA duplex with Hg2+ and consequently enhanced the fluorescence of SG. Meanwhile, TdT and Hg2+ are also the indispensable components of the system, indicating the ability of our method to detect TdT and Hg2+. There are several distinct characteristics for our novel ALP detection assay: (1) the operation and instrument requirement are quite simple; (2) the method is label-free, reliable, convenient, and highly sensitive; (3) the system is multi-functional that could be applied to detect ALP, TdT, and Hg2+ synchronously; (4) the method is quite sensitive for ALP detection that just 0.025 mU mL−1ALP can be easily detected; (5) the results show the potential application of the assay in biological samples.

2′-Deoxythymidine-5′-triphosphate (dTTP), Pfu Protease S, PrimeSTAR® HS DNA Polymerase, T4 DNA Ligase, T4 DNA Polymerase, Alkaline phosphatase (ALP, from calf intestine) and 10 × Alkaline Phosphatase Buffer (500 mM Tris-HCl, pH 9.0, 10 mM MgCl2) were obtained from TaKaRa Biotechnology (Dalian, China). Terminal transferase (TdT), 10 × TdT buffer [500 mM KAc, 200 mM Tris-Ac, 100 mM Mg(Ac)2, pH 7.9], and 2.5 mM CoCl2 solution were purchased from New England Biolabs (Beijing, China). Lysozyme, trypsin, pepsin, and glucose oxidase (GOX) were obtained from Sigma-Aldrich. Na3VO4 was purchased from Macklin (Guangzhou, China). Human serum (HS) was from Solarbio (Guangzhou, China). All the oligo nucleotides used in this study were synthesized by Sangon Biotech Co., Ltd. (Shanghai, China). Diluted water used in this study was purified by a Milli-Q apparatus (Millipore, Bedford, MA).

All fluorescence measurements were performed using a Hitachi F-7000 fluorescence spectrophotometer at 25 °C. The emission spectra were collected from 500 to 600 nm with an excitation wavelength of 494 nm. The slit widths for excitation and emission were 10 nm and 5 nm, respectively.

All of samples were prepared on ice box and stored at −20 °C. The reaction system consisting of 5 μM ssDNA-P, 1 × reaction buffer [20 mM Tris-HAc, 50 mM KAc, 10 mM Mg(Ac)2, pH 7.9], and ALP at various concentrations were incubated at 37 °C for 80 min. Then ALP was deactivated at 90 °C for 10 min and then cooled the sample solutions to room temperature for further monitoring. The following reaction solutions containing 5 unit TdT which was more economy and got better efficacy, 1 × TdT working buffer, 250 μM CoCl2, 0.75 mM dTTPs and the above solutions were kept at 37 °C for 70 min and then TdT was deactivated at 72 °C for 10 min. Various quantities of HgCl2 were added and well mixed with other components in the reaction system for 20 min to allow the formation of T-Hg2+-T configuration. At last, SG was applied to the system at a final concentration of 0.245 μM to detect the fluorescence intensity immediately.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Selectivity and inhibition of the ALP detection assay
ce:section.ce:para: To investigate the selectivity of the proposed alkaline phosphatase activity assay, four physiological enzymes (20 U mL−1 GOX, 20 U mL−1 pepsin, 20 U mL−1 lysozyme, and 20 U mL−1 trypsin) were selected as the potential interfering enzymes with 2.5 U mL−1 ALP in the same procedure. The procedures of inhibition assay were the same as ALP activity assay in “2.3” except that 2.5 U mL−1 ALP and different concentrations of Na3VO4 were added.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Assay of ALP activity in human serum and cell lysates samples
ce:section.ce:para: The procedures of detecting ALP in human serum samples were the same as ALP activity assay in “2.3” except that ALP was replaced by different quantities of human serum (0.2–10%). The detection of ALP in cell lysates was conducted by MCF-7 cells, because they express the ALP positively. When the confluence of MCF-7 was reached 90%, the cells were harvested and centrifuged to obtain the cell precipitation. Then 500 μL PBS was added, and the MCF-7 cells were transformed to cell lysates by several freeze-thaw cycles. A series of cell lysates were added to the system with the final detection concentrations of 2–40 μg mL−1, and the following procedures were same as “2.3”.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Principle and feasibility of the developed assay
ce:section.ce:para: ALP is a member of glycoprotein enzymes and can catalyze the dephosphorylation process of nucleic acids. And TdT is an amazing tool enzyme that can randomly incorporate dTTPs to the 3′-OH termini of ssDNA chains in a template-independent manner. Here, we proposed a new label-free strategy for ALP detection considering the properties of these two enzymes and T-Hg-T structure. As illustrated in Scheme 1, ALP eliminated the phosphate group of ssDNA-p so that the exposed 3′-OH termini of the ssDNA could be elongated by TdT with dTTPs. Then these new produced poly-T tailed sequences would form stable DNA duplex with Hg2+ by forming T-Hg2+-T structure. At first, we checked the elongation ability of TdT by electrophoresis shift assay and the electromobility of ssDNA-2 was delayed obviously with the increasing of TdT (Fig. S1), which was consistent with previous researchs. Then we conducted a series of experiments on CD spectrometer to verify the formation of T-Hg2+-T in our system. As shown in Fig. S2, the negative peak of the ssDNA-3 shifted from 250 nm to 280 nm gradually with the increase of Hg2+, indicating the formation of T-Hg2+-T mediated metallo DNA duplex. Then we performed several fluorescent experiments to demonstrate the feasibility of ALP detection in our strategy ( Fig. 1a). Compared the results of line 2 and 5, only with ALP, could the ssDNA-p be prolonged by TdT, which enhanced fluorescence signal of SG. These demonstrated the feasibility of the proposed strategy.

Especially, only when the ALP, TdT, and Hg2+ presented simultaneously, could the fluorescence signal be obviously enhanced. In view of this, we cited an AND logic gates to exhibit the necessity of coexistence of ALP, TdT, and Hg2+ in our detection system. It's particularly intriguing to adopt enzyme-based logic gate since numerous biocatalytic cycles in nature rely on information processing just like computer devices. The enzyme-mediated logic gates utilize the specificity of enzymatic biocatalytic reactions, which could allow multiple simultaneous reactions. Here, the input signal “1” means the existence of the experimental element; on the contrary, the input signal “0” suggests the absence of it. As illustrated in Fig. 1b, only all input channels were “1”, would the system yield output signal “1”. If anyone of these three elements input signal “0”, there would yield output signal “0”. Here, the digitally encoded output signal “1” is considered to have higher fluorescence intensity than the output signal “0”. Our logic gate is in accordance with fluorescence results in Fig. 1a.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Optimization of performance conditions for accurately ALP detection
ce:section.ce:para: To achieve the high sensitivity of the system, involved factors in this assay were optimized. Firstly, we optimized the quantity of ssDNA-p and the ratio of dTTP/ssDNA-p. As expected, with the ratio of dTTP/ssDNA-p increased, the fluorescence signal showed exponential growth. In order to avoid the data fluctuation and obtain a better accuracy, we fixed the ratio of 5000 (dTTP/ssDNA-p) for further experiments (Fig. S3). Besides, incubation time is a vital factor for enzyme efficiency, so TdT incubation time and ALP dephosphorylation reaction time were optimized respectively. From the results, the fluorescent intensity reached to a plateau when the incubation time was 70 min and 80 min correspondingly (Fig. S4). So we fixed the ratio of 5000 (dTTP/ssDNA-p) and 2.5 μM Hg2+ for further experiments, and 70 min and 80 min were chosen as the optimal reaction time for TdT and ALP. And all these operations were simple and convenient.

Under the optimal experimental conditions, the performance of this fluorescence enhancing assay was examined by varying concentrations of ALP. As depicted in Fig. 2a, the reaction mixture exhibited strong fluorescence emission intensity at 530 nm, and the fluorescence emission intensity arised with the increase of ALP activity. Correspondingly, Fig. 2b showed that our method possessed a wide detection range from 0 to 2500 mU mL−1, and the inset picture denoted a good linear correlation of the logarithm of ALP concentration over the range of 0.025-2500 mU mL−1. Notably, the actual lowest detection limit for our assay was 0.025 mU mL−1, demonstrating that this assay for detecting ALP was superior to those of recent reported methods. The low detection limit may bring benefit to a low sampling quantity, which has great merits in further clinical application.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Selectivity and inhibitor screening of ALP
ce:section.ce:para: To further evaluate the specificity and selectivity of the ALP assays, several physiological enzymes, including lysozyme, trypsin, pepsin, and glucose oxidase (GOX), were selected as the interfering enzymes to assess the selectivity of this method. As results presented in Fig. 3a, other enzymes didn’t show the interference in fluorescence intensity under the same condition, even at 8-fold higher concentrations than that of ALP. Therefore, the proposed assay exhibited good performance for discriminating ALP against other interfering enzymes.

The inhibition of ALP is closely correlated with drug screening and disease therapy, so we investigated the validity of the proposed assay in evaluation of ALP inhibition. Na3VO4, as a competitive inhibitor of ALP [55], was chosen as a model inhibitor in our system. As shown in Fig. 3b, Na3VO4 displayed a dose-dependent inhibition behavior for ALP and the IC50 value (half maximal inhibitory concentration of the inhibitor) was 50.9 μM (Fig. 3c). These findings reveal that the proposed strategy is capable of ALP inhibitor efficiency evaluation, and has the potential in the screening of ALP inhibitors in medical practice.

ce:section.ce:section-title: ALP activity assay in biological fluid
ce:section.ce:para: To evaluate the effectiveness and suitability of our developed assay for a complex sample system, we further tested the performance of this fluorescence enhancing assay in the human serum samples and in MCF-7 cells lysates to analysis its relevant practical application. As shown in Fig. S6a, a good linear relationship between the fluorescence enhancement and different quantities of human serum was obtained over the range from 0.2% to 6%. Similarly in Fig. 4a, the fluorescence intensity enhanced with the increasing the amount of cell lysates, indicating the enhancement of ALP activity. And a wide linear relationship was obtained in the range of 2–40 μg mL−1. The results verified that our proposed efficient system possessed the potential to monitor the ALP activity in biological samples and complex matrix. Moreover, we carried out several experiments to investigate the detection specificity for ALP, and the results were presented in Fig. 4b and Fig. S6b. In the presence of Na3VO4, fluorescence intensity was significantly decreased in human serum and MCF-7 cells lysates, confirming that our assay was specially targeted ALP in biological samples.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Further application for TdT and Hg2+ detection
ce:section.ce:para: As a biomarker for leukemic disease and a nucleic acid modifying tool, the detection of TdT activity has clinical and molecular biological significance [56]. Surprisingly, our assay system exhibited the ability to detect TdT, which was directly demonstrated in Fig. 5a, the fluorescent response increased gradually with the increment of TdT, and a good linear response was observed when the amounts of TdT ranged from 0 to 5 U. The detection limit of TdT was as low as 0.01U giving a signal-to-noise (S/N) ratio of 3. What's more, the selectivity of the proposed system for TdT detection was evaluated by investigating fluorescent intensity of the biosensing system by adding TdT or other DNA polymerases, including PFU, PrimerStar, T4 DNA polymerase, and T4 DNA ligase. As shown in Fig. 5b, only TdT could induce a remarkable fluorescence enhancement, while other enzymes had no obvious fluorescence change, even when adding 10-fold higher concentrations than TdT, indicating that the system for TdT activity assay exhibited excellent selectivity against other DNA polymerases.

Since the early and increasing usage of mercury in our society has resulted in an increase in environmental contamination, detection of mercury is of growing significance [57]. Similarly, our new assay could be further employed in detection of trace amount of mercury. As presented in Fig. 5c, when the quantities of Hg2+ ranged from 125 to 1250 nM, a good linear response was obtained and the detection limit was calculated to be 22.97 nM based on signal to noise of 3. Furthermore, to assess the selectivity of the presented method for Hg2+ detection, a range of possible other metal ions, including Ca2+, Mg2+, Mn2+, Pb2+, Ba2+, Cd2+, Ni2+, Cu2+, Zn2+, and Sr2+, were studied at a consistent concentration of 2.5 μM each. Fig. 5d showed the high selectivity of our method for Hg2+ over the other metal ions, which was attributable to highly specific T-Hg2+-T structure. The result suggested that our approach could be applied to detect Hg2+.

In summary, a versatile assay was designed for highly sensitive detection of the activity and inhibition of ALP. In the system, the ssDNA-p was dephosphorylated by ALP and equipped with a poly-T sequence via the action of TdT. Then, the ssDNA with poly-T tail could form stable DNA duplex with the addition of Hg2+ and the new formed DNA duplex caused the obvious enhancement of the fluorescence intensity of SG. Compared to existed methods, the developed assay has the advantages of versatile, label-free, simple operation, low cost, wide linear range and low detection limit. The good sensitivity enabled the assay for evaluating ALP activities in human serum and MCF-7 cell lysates. This study may find wide applications in clinical diagnosis, in biomedical research of dephosphorylation relevant process and in pharmaceutical industry for screening enzymatic inhibitors as drugs. The proposed strategy was further applied to the detection of TdT and Hg2+, which presented a promising and feasible method for TdT activity detection in clinical diagnosis and drug discovery as well as for Hg2+ determination in biosamples. In addition, it can also be extended to the detection of activity of other nucleic acid related enzymes, such as phosphorylase, restriction endonuclease, methylation transferase and so on, only with a little bit change in the probe sequences.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This research was supported by grants from National Natural Science Foundation of China (No. 21708014, No. 21708005, No. 81672006), Natural Science Foundation of Guangdong Province, China (No. 2017A030310487), Guangdong Innovative and Entepreneurial Research Team Program (No. 2014ZT05S136), Program for Cultivate and Innovation from Jinan University in Guangzhou (No. 21616302).

ce:section.ce:para: There are no conflicts to declare.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data associated with this article can be found in the online version at doi:10.1016/j.talanta.2018.11.061.
